selected_important_turn.text: I'm so sorry, we're getting very close to the end and will need to make a decision very soon!  We can go without any firewood so How about you get all 2 firewood, 2 water and 1 food.  I will really need at least 2 packages of food for all of us.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn crystallizes the division of the highest-value resource (food) in the speaker's favor, uses urgency to pressure agreement, and directly precedes the opponent's acceptance — it determines the deal outcome and relational tone.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's coercive and zero-sum: after earlier cooperative gestures, the speaker reasserts a firm self-interested minimum and frames urgency, which risks harming fairness perceptions and relationship. It also forecloses integrative tradeoffs (e.g., sharing, contingencies, or compensations) that could raise joint value.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Preserve the goal of securing needed food but drop the urgency and the hard-minimum framing; acknowledge the opponent's stated needs, propose explicit tradeoffs or conditional offers (e.g., "I need 2 food; would you accept 2 firewood + 2 water if I take 2 food and we split the remaining food differently / share one meal?), or offer a small compensation (extra water) in return for the distribution. Invite a brief check that the opponent can live with the arrangement rather than pressuring immediate assent.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — opening to tradeoffs or conditional packages creates more integrative options and may find a win-win split or compensations.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — removing urgency and offering explicit exchanges signals respect for the opponent's needs and makes outcomes feel more equitable.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: slightly decrease short-term (more discussion) but increase long-term — a brief additional exchange can delay a pressured close but make agreement more durable and acceptable.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — reducing pressure and showing willingness to trade builds trust and rapport.

value2reason.Low: Water is essential to survival. It is necessary to drink, cook and bathe in.
value2reason.Medium: Firewood could get wet and we would need more to stay warm at night and on cold days. It is also important for cooking.
value2reason.High: We will be hungry and food is essential for survival.

value2reason.Low: While we can make fire with what we have, additional wood would be good in case what we have gets wet
value2reason.Medium: While we do have some water, the weather is looking to be especially hot and we will probably need more as we are looking to do a lot of hiking and kayaking.
value2reason.High: I have two teenage boys who require a lot of food, especially when expending so much energy with all the activities of camping.

dialogue.text: Hi.  I am looking at possibly taking 3 of the food packages, 2 of the water packages and one of the firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hi. Why do you need so many food packages?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Well, I have two teenage boys who will be with me.  In normal circumstances they go through a lot of food.  While camping, hiking and kayaking I know we will need plenty to keep their energy level high to do everything.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well I will definitely need 2 packages of food and 2 of firewood for survival.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I am definitely ok with giving you two of the firewood!!  Why do you need 2 packages of food?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I have a very fast metabolism and a dog to take care of. Thanks for the firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Awe!  We have a dog as well, I love dogs!!  How about if you get 1 package of food, 1 water and 2 firewood?  Would that be sufficient for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I know I will need at least 2 packages of food. But you can have 2 of water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm so sorry, we're getting very close to the end and will need to make a decision very soon!  We can go without any firewood so How about you get all 2 firewood, 2 water and 1 food.  I will really need at least 2 packages of food for all of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 food packages. The boys will need them. If we split the remaining food, I'd be happy to trade some water or firewood. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to give you 2 food packages. For the remaining food, I suggest splitting 1 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water with you. This way, we both get essential supplies while respecting our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with 2 food, 1 water, and 1 firewood. That gives me enough for the boys and covers the heat. Let's confirm the split and move on.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Let's confirm the split: I get 2 food, 2 firewood, and 2 water. You get 1 food, 1 firewood, and 1 water. This balances our needs and ensures we're both prepared for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Let’s finalize the split: I take 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. You get 1 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood. This works for me and covers our needs. Ready to agree?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to the split: you get 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood, and I get 1 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood. It’s fair and covers our needs. Let’s confirm and move on.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Let’s confirm the final split: I take 2 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. You get 1 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood. It’s fair and covers our needs. Ready to agree?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
